ps140: 4, Podoben “As one valiant among the martyrs…”

You were an invincible commander,* a valiant athlete, and a universal beacon,* illumining the ends of the world with the dawning of your miracles* and dispelling the heavy darkness of the demons,* O great Sabbas, adornment of martyrs,* fellow citizen of the holy incorporeal ones,* who prays for our souls.

Incarcerated in prisons, burned with fire,* tortured with many torments, torn asunder upon a tree,* afflicted with winters cold,* and unceasingly inflicted with frequent wounds,* you remained unshaken, O athlete,* receiving with fervour a blessed end in the waters.

You were a comely youth, O you who are most rich, ever marvelled at by all;* you fearlessly set yourself apart for the labours of suffering,* and with your holy and great struggles* raised up standards of victory against all enemies.* Wherefore, O right wondrous one, we honour you in praise.
theotokion: With the outpourings of the all-holy Spirit* bedew my thought, O you who gave birth to Christ, the Drop* who, by His compassions, has washed away the infinitely countless iniquities of men.* Dry up the well-spring of my passions,* and, by your supplications, ever vouchsafe to me* a torrent of living nourishment.
stavrotheotokion: When she saw You, the Lamb and Shepherd, upon the Tree,* the ewe-lamb who gave You birth lamented and maternally exclaimed to You:* “O my Son most desired,* how is it that You are hanging upon the tree of the Cross,* nailed by the iniquitous,* and have shed Your blood, O Master?”
